title: EDR auth + authz
description: Operator dashboard for the wave-1 auth + authz surface. Imported into SigNoz via Dashboards -> Import. Every successful audit row is dual-emitted to slog at INFO with stable attribute keys (action, target_type, target_id, actor_email, edr.user.id, optional trace_id + payload), and the async writer emits structured slog WARNs on drops + INSERT errors. Panel queries below assume the EDR server is exporting via OTLP with service.name=fleet-edr-server. SigNoz's import dialog re-numbers panels; the layout block is a starting hint only.

panels.title: Authz deny rate by reason
panels.description: Count of audit-row INFO logs whose action starts with authz. and whose payload.decision = deny, grouped by payload.reason. The chokepoint emits exactly one such row per privileged HTTP request (allow + deny), so this panel is a faithful proxy for chokepoint deny rate. A baseline of low-volume reauth_required denies is healthy (operators triggering the freshness gate); a sudden spike on role_missing or actor_anonymous is worth chasing. Cross-reference with HTTP traces (status_code=403) to attribute denies to specific routes.

panels.title: Login outcomes (OIDC + break-glass)
panels.description: Stack of successful and failed logins across both auth surfaces. Reads directly from audit-row INFO logs: OIDC emits auth.oidc.success / auth.oidc.failure / auth.oidc.callback.error; break-glass emits auth.breakglass.success / auth.breakglass.failure on the day-to-day login flow. A flat success line plus a sustained failure line means brute-force or a misconfig; a failure spike with no success spike often means the IdP is down.

panels.title: Break-glass redemption rate
panels.description: Count of auth.breakglass.bootstrap audit rows. Emitted on successful redemption (token issuance itself is not audited), so this panel charts how often the operator completes the WebAuthn-mandatory setup flow. A non-zero rate outside a deploy window means a manual SQL recovery insert was redeemed; a sustained zero with a known token outstanding means the operator never finished the ceremony.

panels.title: Audit-write failures (drops + INSERT errors)
panels.description: The async audit writer emits a slog WARN when it cannot enqueue (queue_full / writer_stopped / drain_deadline_exceeded) or when an INSERT into audit_events fails. Any non-zero rate here is a hard alert: the audit log is the spec's append-only invariant and dropping rows breaks it. Drop logs carry a 'reason' attribute; INSERT-failure logs carry an 'err' attribute instead. Grouping by body cleanly splits the two cases.
